response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented full-stack engineer with genuine systems-programming depth (low-latency DLang services, async I/O, event-driven architectures), but his professional C++ experience amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments nearly two decades ago. The role demands current, hands-on C++/STL expertise plus industrial-automation domain knowledge — MQTT, IEC 62443 SDL practices, OT protocols — none of which appear on the resume. His recent decade is TypeScript/Node.js product and AI-tooling work, making this a substantial retooling rather than a natural fit.
strengths: Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency, multi-threaded, event-driven services handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering craft — TDD, CI/CD, testing frameworks, plus mentoring and workshop leadership matching the role's mentoring duties
Broad polyglot background spanning embedded, desktop, web, and mobile, with some C/C++ exposure
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ (2007–2008); no evidence of modern C++/STL depth the role centers on
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, or IEC 62443 / secure development lifecycle experience
Recent focus (2018–present) is full-stack TypeScript and AI tooling, far from on-prem industrial platform development
